Help & Guided Learning

Learn only what you need

Guided-learning progress

Completed lessons stay checked on this browser. Exiting a lesson saves your place without changing normal planner workflows.

Print Options

Print View

Clean names only removes classroom styling. Substitute print also uses the clean layout, then includes only the note categories selected below.

Student Details to Print

Names print on seats. Grade and ID print as smaller detail lines under the name.

Chart Details to Print

These fields come from Settings > Seating Chart Details and print above the seating chart.

Notes to Include

Only selected note categories appear on the print page. Private notes stay off unless you explicitly include them here.

Configuration Sections

Group and Rule Details

Zone Details

Turn on the Group or Zone section, then choose exactly which details print. Names still print as the card headings so the section remains readable even when only a few details are selected.

PDF and SVG Page Framing

For PDF and SVG output, trims unused outer Grid cells or Freeform canvas space, then enlarges the remaining seats and room objects to use the printable page while keeping the complete room on one chart page. Notes, Groups, and Zones may still use supporting pages.

Download Output Copies

PDF and SVG are printable/shareable output copies. They are not editable save files and they do not replace your save file or backup.

CSV Import

Choose a CSV file, then map its columns to First Name, Last Name, Nickname, Grade, ID, and categorized notes before importing.

Student List 0

Use this list to manage roster records, notes, rules, and today's attendance. Seat placement happens in the Seat Students stage, where the chart is visible.

Create Group or Seating Rule

Existing Groups & Rules 0

Create and manage memberships here. Seat anchors are assigned later in Room Design or Seat Students, when the chart is visible.

Create / Apply Zone

Select seats in the Room Layout, then save them as a named zone. Zones can also be connected to students and groups in the manager.

Generate and Randomize now compare seating options before changing the chart.
Use this stage to build the room: choose Grid or Freeform, add seats and room objects, then use Box Select, Group, Lock Group, alignment, and audit tools to organize the layout. Student placement happens in Seat Students, where the chart and unassigned roster are visible together. Generate and Randomize preserve locked placements and use the actual Freeform seat positions.

Student / Group / Zone Manager

Drag students into groups or zones. Drag groups onto zones, or zones onto groups, to attach that group to a preferred zone. Drag a group member to the remove area to take that student out of that group. On touch devices, use Pick Up and then tap a destination.

Freeform Object Presets

Insert an editable group, then move, resize, ungroup, or relabel it like any other Freeform objects.

Search Planner

Offline Recovery Kit

The recovery kit creates a separately encrypted full backup and a one-time recovery key. Store the two downloads in different secure locations. Anyone with both can read the planner data.
To restore laterChoose Restore / Upload Classes File, select the recovery JSON file, and enter the separate recovery key when prompted.

About Classroom Seating Planner

A privacy-conscious classroom planning workspace for building rosters, designing rooms, generating seating plans, reviewing constraints, and creating purpose-specific print and sharing outputs.

Written byChris L. Franklin Optional support

Classroom Seating Planner is free to use. Donations are entirely optional, never expected, and do not unlock features. This button appears only in About.

PayPal donation button loads when this About section is opened.
VersionLoading... Release dateLoading... BuildLoading... Data formatLoading... LicenseLoading... StorageRuns locally in the browser. Use a linked save file or downloaded backup for durable storage.

Project Features

The portable release is self-contained and keeps student data in the browser unless the user explicitly saves or exports it. The hosted PWA uses same-origin assets for managed deployment and offline installation. Optional Google Drive support uses Google Identity Services and the Drive API; no third-party application runtime framework is bundled.

Release Notes & Project History

The current release is documented in detail. Earlier point releases are grouped into milestones so the app keeps a useful history without carrying a novel inside the JavaScript.

License

LicenseLoading... SPDX IDLoading... Copyright holderLoading... Copyright yearLoading...
This notice is embedded in the app file and included in complete save/export files as app metadata. It does not claim ownership over student or classroom data entered by users.

Class Name

Settings

Chart details Title, date, room, and printable chart identity.

Seating Chart Details

Appearance and defaults Theme, default layout state, hints, and visibility-mode display choices.

Visual Theme

Themes change the overall feel and colors without changing seating data. Prismatic Flow adds animated jewel-tone gradients and glass surfaces; reduced-motion settings automatically calm the animation. The object-label option hides the small Seat, Teacher Desk, Table, and custom-object type indicators without hiding student names or custom seat labels.

Page Load Defaults

These defaults save with linked, downloaded, and uploaded class files. They apply when a save is loaded or when you select Apply Defaults to Current View, so changing a checkbox does not rearrange the open Settings window. If there are no students, Add Student opens automatically even when its default is collapsed.
Help and guides Search the reference guide, start Quick Start, or open a focused hands-on lesson from one reliable place on desktop and mobile.

Help Tools

On smaller screens, use this page instead of hunting for a header icon. Every help route remains available here.

What each tool does

Quick StartAn eight-step overview of creating a class, building the room, seating students, reviewing, saving, and sharing.
Hands-on lessonsPractice Freeform tools, manual placement, random seating, candidate generation, groups, zones, snapshots, printing, imports, Google connections, security setup, and Drive collaboration.
Practice classesLessons can create a clearly labeled temporary class so users can learn without modifying a real roster or room.
Reference guideSearch explanations, examples, security guidance, sharing roles, password-protected read-only packages, printing help, and troubleshooting without starting a lesson.
Wizard and workflow indexLists every major guided flow, where it starts, what it changes, and which recovery or cancel path protects existing data.
Presentation Mode visibility and protection A locked Review view with encrypted hidden-detail protection and adjustable on-screen seat size.

Presentation Mode

Presentation Mode switches to Review, hides workflow guidance, rosters, chart-review details, undo/redo, Help, snapshots, and editing tools, and keeps only the room display plus its Zoom and Text sliders. The full original chart is encrypted into session storage, the visible in-memory chart is sanitized, and exiting restores the original with the Presentation PIN, Lock PIN, or current encryption key.
Saving and backups Linked save file, auto-save, backups, and save/download encryption.

Save / Download Encryption

Higher values slow password guessing but may make saves/loads slower on older devices. Existing saves/backups keep the iteration count they were created with.
The Welcome screen requires an encryption password before first use. For security, the active session key is not prefilled here after entry. Updating the password changes future local saves, linked saves, backups, and new snapshots only. Older downloaded saves/backups/snapshots remain encrypted with the password used when they were created. The key is not written into exported save files; while Lock or Presentation Mode is active, it can be stored only as an encrypted wrapped key protected by the Lock or Presentation Mode PIN/password.

Saving

Browser local auto-save starts by default. Choose a real save file for safer long-term storage; use downloads for portable backups or selected-data transfers.
Save methodChecking...
Save fileBrowser local save active.
Last saveNever
BackupNever
SecurityChecking...
Google DriveNot connected.
StatusReady
Minutes between saves. If no save file is chosen, the app auto-saves to browser local storage and creates local full-app snapshots. Use 0 to turn auto-save off.

Google Drive Storage

Optional cloud storage for encrypted saves. This public build uses the app's built-in Google OAuth Client ID. The page still must be hosted from an authorized HTTPS origin for Google Drive sign-in to work.
Google Drive is not connected.
Security and local data Auto-lock, Settings access, PINs, local data cleanup, and full reset.

Security Status

Active keyChecking...
Storage encryptedChecking...
Settings protectedChecking...
Lock PIN setChecking...
Presentation PIN setChecking...

Lock Immediately After Inactivity

Use 0 to disable return-lock. Example: set 10 so if the tab was hidden for 10+ minutes, it locks as soon as the user returns.
Auto-lock encrypts the current chart state, scrubs visible fields, and requires a Lock PIN/password plus an active session encryption key workflow. Set a Lock PIN/password before relying on unattended auto-lock.

Settings Access

Choose what credential is required before the Settings page opens. By default, the first-run encryption password protects Settings. You can disable the prompt, but doing so lets anyone with the unlocked page change security, visibility, save, and local-data settings. Auto uses Lock PIN first, then Presentation PIN, then the current session encryption key.

Lock / Presentation PINs

PINs are stored as salted PBKDF2-SHA-256 hashes, not plain text. Chart data encryption still uses the encryption key when set, otherwise the lock PIN/password is used as a fallback.

Lock / Unlock PIN

Presentation Mode Exit PIN

Room tools Custom right-click room objects and sample classroom data.

Right-Click Room Objects

Add, rename, or delete custom room objects used in the Click Tool and right-click cell menu. Built-in objects like Wall, Door, Board, Projector, Carpet, and ADA Space remain available.

Sample Data

Loads sample students, groups, anchors, and a starter room layout into the current class.
About this app Revision, license, project features, and change history.

About

A privacy-conscious classroom planning workspace for building rosters, designing rooms, generating seating plans, reviewing constraints, and creating purpose-specific print and sharing outputs.

Written byChris L. Franklin Optional support

Classroom Seating Planner is free to use. Donations are entirely optional, never expected, and do not unlock features. This button appears only in About.

PayPal donation button loads when this About section is opened.
VersionLoading... Release dateLoading... BuildLoading... Data formatLoading... LicenseLoading... StorageRuns locally in the browser. Use a linked save file or downloaded backup for durable storage.

Project Features

Edit Student

Student Notes
No notes defined.

Individual Seating Requirements

Requirements are encrypted with the student record and evaluated by the seating generator. “Require” items are reported as hard conflicts when no valid seat is available.
If you change the ID, group/rule memberships and seat assignments are updated to the new ID. Categorized notes are included in encrypted saves/exports. Clean/student-facing print modes hide private and substitute notes.

Student Notes

Add notes and choose a visibility category.

Edit Seat

Assign User

Assign Group to Seat

Define Cell Type

Full App Snapshots

Save named restore points for the entire data set: all classes, students, groups, zones, room templates, settings, and layouts. Restoring a snapshot first saves a return point of the current app state so you can roll forward again.

Room Templates

Save the current room layout without student assignments, then reuse it in another class.

CSV Import Wizard

Map CSV columns before importing. The preview shows the first few parsed rows. Save a mapping profile when your roster exports use the same headings each time.

Preview

Selected item

Save & Backup Options

Download or restore selected data

Use these when you need a backup, need to move a piece of setup to another class, or need to restore part of a saved file without replacing everything.

Google Drive storage location

Save and load the encrypted full-app save in the user's own Google Drive. This public build uses the app's built-in Google OAuth Client ID, and the encryption password still protects the data before upload.

Google Drive is not connected.
Tip: student data downloads are encrypted automatically when an encryption key is available.

Choose What to Restore

Review the file contents, then choose what should be restored.
Partial restore applies to the currently open class. Matching IDs are updated; new items are added.
Restoring changes current data. A snapshot/undo point is created first.
First-time setup

Welcome to Classroom Seating Planner

About 1 minute

Protect your seating charts

Create one password for encrypted browser autosaves, backups, and save files. The app keeps student data on this device unless you choose another save location.

Create your encryption passwordUse a memorable passphrase. The app cannot recover it unless you later create and separately store an optional recovery package.
Required
Already have a seating chart?Open an existing encrypted save instead of creating a new workspace.

Additional lock, privacy, and auto-lock options are available later under Settings → Security & Data.

Choose a Google Drive Save

Connect to Google Drive to list encrypted saves created by this app.

Please Confirm

Encryption Key Required

This save/export is encrypted. Enter the encryption key to continue.

Settings Locked

Enter the required credential to open Settings.
🔒

Seating Chart Locked

Editing is paused. Enter the PIN/password used to lock this page to continue.

Lock and Encrypt Page

Enter the page lock PIN/password. The active chart data will be encrypted with this PIN/password while the page is locked.

Set Lock PIN/Password

Create the PIN/password that will be required to unlock this page. It will be stored only as a salted cryptographic hash, not as plain text.

Exit Presentation Mode

Enter the Presentation Mode PIN, Lock PIN, or current encryption key to restore the previous workflow and editing controls.

Enter Protected Presentation Mode

Enter the Presentation PIN or Lock PIN/password. Presentation Mode encrypts the full original chart state, opens a read-only Review workspace, shows only the configured basic information, and restores the original workflow when you exit.

Set Presentation Mode PIN

No Presentation Mode PIN, Lock PIN, or encryption key is currently available. Create a Presentation Mode PIN/password. It is stored only as a salted cryptographic hash.
